title: Auto-(de)phosphorylation assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"710k6","text":"This protocol describes an assay to measure ATP13A4 phospho-enzyme levels. ATP13A4, a P-type ATPase, forms a phosphorylated intermediate on a conserved aspartate residue during its catalytic cycle. In P5B ATPases like ATP13A4, spontaneous auto-phosphorylation occurs, followed by polyamine-induced dephosphorylation. The assay utilizes SDS-PAGE under acidic conditions, preserving the stability of the aspartyl-phosphate intermediate, followed by autoradiography to detect [32P]-labeled phosphorylated intermediates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":474,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38gga","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ejv31","text":"The assay must be conducted in a designated radioactive area by authorized personnel, adhering strictly to all radioactive material handling precautions. Appropriate personal protective equipment, including gloves, lab goggles, lab coats, and a dosimeter for monitoring exposure, must be worn at all times. Work should be carried out behind Plexiglass screens, and only filter-equipped pipette tips should be used. Institutional regulations must be followed for the storage of radioactive materials and the disposal of both solid and liquid radioactive waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c0u6g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare mixes for running gels and stacking gels: Components Running gel Stacking gel 1 30% acrylamide/bis-acrylamide 10 ml 1.6 ml 2 H20 24.6 ml 8.52 ml 3 *TEMED 30 ul 19.2 ul 4 *APS 10% 300 ul 200 ul 5 SDS 10% 400 ul 120 ul 6 Running gel buffer 5 ml N/A 7 Stacking gel buffer N/A 1.52 ml * Add these components right before pouring the gel!
Pour running gel in the glass plates assembled with spacers.
Add butanol on top of the running gel and wait until the running gel is solidified to wash the butanol off with water.
Pour stacking gel on top and insert a suitable comb.
Add membranes ( 40 µg protein) or purified protein ( 1 µg ), 10 µL 10x EP buffer solution, milliQ water and whatever you need (compound to test) in a final volume of 95 µL . Keep samples on ice!
To start the reaction: add 5 µL radioactive ATP mix. Vortex.
To prepare radioactive ATP mix (2 μCi, 5.125 micromolar (µM) ), pipet together 5 µL [γ- 32 P] ATP + 110.5 µL 120 micromolar (µM) cold ATP
For phosphorylation only: stop reaction with 400 µL stop solution after desired time.
Leave samples on ice for 00:30:00 to precipitate protein.
Aspirate supernatant with pipet, keep in disposable recipient.
Wash pellet with stop solution ( 400 µL ), and 20.000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:10:00 .
Repeat step 12 and 13.
Dissolve pellet in 40 µL sample buffer.
Load 40 µL on acidic gels. Very important to load gel carefully, avoid overflow between wells!
Run the gel at 100 V, 04:00:00 4 °C .
When the gel run is complete, carefully cut off the blue dye front. This is excess 32 P running ahead of the samples and is not related to ATP13A4 phosphoenzyme which runs at 130 kDa.
Soak gel for 00:05:00 in 7.5% acetic acid, and put gel in plastic bag.
Expose gel Overnight to phosphoimager cassette.
Image cassette using a phosphor imager ( e.g. Typhoon‱ FLA 7000, GE Healthcare).
To verify equal sample loading, soak the gel in Coomassie stain Overnight , then wash and evaluate the staining intensity.
For dephosphorylation: 30 s after addition of [γ- 32 P] ATP, add compound to test ( e.g. non-radioactive ATP, ADP and/or polyamines) and stop reaction with 400 µL stop solution after desired time.
Once the stacking gel is solidified, store at 4 °C .
Wash pellet with 0.3 M hydroxylamine (HA) ( 200 µL ), incubate for 00:15:00 and 20.000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:10:00 .
creator: Sarah van Veen
